question: Using a class-transformation gradient-boosting uplift model with the existing rfm_baseline features will increase uplift AUC relative to the current two-model logistic regression benchmark.
hypothesis_text: Using a class-transformation gradient-boosting uplift model with the existing rfm_baseline features will increase uplift AUC relative to the current two-model logistic regression benchmark.
expected_signal: The only completed run (two-model logistic regression) shows minimal uplift signal (uplift AUC ≈ 0.045) yet no over-fitting (hold-out Qini > train). This under-fitting pattern suggests capacity limits of the linear model; tree-based gradient boosting is well-documented to capture non-linear interactions across RFM, basket, and points variables and is available in the allowed model pair list but has not been tried.

question: Ablating the dominant demographic columns (e.g., age_clean, issue_year, issue_month) and re-training the two-model logistic regression will raise held-out Qini/AUC, because it removes a spurious but highly weighted signal that appears to drive over-fitting and weak generalisation.
hypothesis_text: Ablating the dominant demographic columns (e.g., age_clean, issue_year, issue_month) and re-training the two-model logistic regression will raise held-out Qini/AUC, because it removes a spurious but highly weighted signal that appears to drive over-fitting and weak generalisation.
expected_signal: Both the baseline two-model LR and the class-transformation GBM show age_clean and issue_year/month among the top 5 XAI drivers, yet the more flexible GBM deteriorated on hold-out (Qini 294.7 < 309.8), suggesting the model family is latching onto cohort effects rather than genuine treatment heterogeneity. Eliminating these features should force the learner to rely on recency, frequency, points burn-rate and basket interactions that are more causally plausible.

question: Training a class_transformation + XGBoost uplift model on the hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 features (demographics still excluded) will exceed the current two_model-XGBoost champion on held-out Qini.
hypothesis_text: Training a class_transformation + XGBoost uplift model on the hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 features (demographics still excluded) will exceed the current two_model-XGBoost champion on held-out Qini.
expected_signal: The two_model-XGBoost run is the incumbent (Qini≈310). A prior class_transformation trial failed only when paired with vanilla GBM and the weaker RFM-only feature set. XGBoost already shows stronger signal than GBM in the two-model setting, and literature reports that class_transformation with a powerful learner can reduce variance versus the two-model approach when rich behavioural features are present.

question: Class-transformation LightGBM paired with the current behavioural feature recipe will surpass the existing best held-out Qini, thanks to LightGBM’s leaf-wise tree growth capturing high-order interactions with fewer trees and less regularisation bias than XGBoost.
hypothesis_text: Class-transformation LightGBM paired with the current behavioural feature recipe will surpass the existing best held-out Qini, thanks to LightGBM’s leaf-wise tree growth capturing high-order interactions with fewer trees and less regularisation bias than XGBoost.
expected_signal: Class-transformation + XGBoost on hybrid_safe_semantic_v1 is the present top performer (held-out Qini≈326). No LightGBM run with the same recipe exists yet; in other marketing uplift tasks LightGBM has edged out XGBoost when rich behavioural features were available because its leaf-wise splits better exploit sparse, high-cardinality baskets and points ratios while avoiding the variance observed in two-model XGBoost (held-out Qini dropped to 310).

question: Swapping the XGBoost learner for LightGBM within the class_transformation architecture will further raise held-out Qini and uplift-AUC because LightGBM’s leaf-wise growth can exploit the rich behavioural feature space more efficiently than XGBoost’s level-wise splits.
hypothesis_text: Swapping the XGBoost learner for LightGBM within the class_transformation architecture will further raise held-out Qini and uplift-AUC because LightGBM’s leaf-wise growth can exploit the rich behavioural feature space more efficiently than XGBoost’s level-wise splits.
expected_signal: class_transformation + XGBoost already beats two_model + XGBoost by ≈16 Qini points and keeps uplift-AUC >0.06. LightGBM has not yet been tested with class_transformation, and in many tabular uplift benchmarks it outperforms XGBoost when abundant numeric aggregates (recency, frequency, points ratios, basket counts) are present.
